title: Non-Hermitian Twisting Theory under the open boundary condition
authors: Chen-Hao Zhao, Jia-Rui Li, Yuping Tian, Wei-Jiang Gong
abstract: The non-Hermitian skin effect (NHSE) is a hallmark of non-Hermitian system, yet its generalized Brillouin zone (GBZ) description is restricted to periodic systems. We develop a site-resolved theory via a local scaling transformation (LST), introducing local twisting $T_n$ to quantify metric operator $ξ$ nontriviality. This elucidates the NHSE's origin and uncovers the generalized multiple-channel skin effect (MCSE). Exploiting $T_n$'s translational independence, we define the Zahlen-Brillouin Zone (ZBZ), extending non-Hermitian band theory to nonperiodic and disordered lattices. Furthermore, we unify the $ξ$ with GBZ Riemannian geometry, establishing the metric and state correspondence (MSC) as the principle for real-space localization. With a global skin index $\mathbfΓ$ for phase transitions, our results provide a universal paradigm for non-Hermitian physics in both crystalline and amorphous media.
summary: This paper extends the theory of the Non-Hermitian Skin Effect to nonperiodic and disordered systems using a new site-resolved 'twisting' framework. It introduces the Zahlen-Brillouin Zone and discovers a multiple-channel skin effect arising from competing nonreciprocal sites. This provides a universal way to diagnose non-Hermitian physics in both crystalline and amorphous media.
main_problem: The Generalized Brillouin Zone (GBZ) description of the Non-Hermitian Skin Effect (NHSE) is limited to translationally invariant systems, failing to account for localization in disordered, quasiperiodic, or amorphous lattices.
main_result: The authors develop a site-resolved theory that introduces the Zahlen-Brillouin Zone (ZBZ) and identifies a Multiple-Channel Skin Effect (MCSE) where competing nonreciprocal sites lead to complex localization patterns.
method: A Local Scaling Transformation (LST) is used to absorb nonreciprocity into coordinates, alongside a new metric-state correspondence (MSC) principle and a local lattice twisting parameter (Tn).
model_or_system: Generic nonreciprocal lattices, including periodic, nonperiodic, and quasi-periodic (modulated) systems, described by a Hamiltonian with asymmetric intracell hoppings.
key_observables: Local lattice twisting (Tn), Global skin index (Gamma), and the Maximal Normalized Cumulative Twisting Weight.
important_parameters: Unidirectional Twisting (UT) regime, Competing Twisting (CT) regime, and the Antagonistic vs. Complete phases.
main_assumptions: The theory assumes the existence of a metric operator and a local scaling transformation that can map the non-Hermitian Hamiltonian to a Hermitian counterpart.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the LST and ZBZ discretization; Figure 2 shows the localization profiles; Figure 3 displays the skin index and ZBZ distribution for non-periodic and quasi-periodic models.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the limitation of GBZ, develops the LST and ZBZ framework, defines the local twisting parameter, classifies NHSE phases (UT vs CT), and establishes the metric-state correspondence.
why_interesting: It provides a universal framework for understanding non-Hermitian localization in the absence of translational symmetry, which is highly relevant for studying open quantum systems and non-equilibrium dynamics in disordered environments.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Schematic of lattice twisting and the Zahlen Bril- louin zone (ZBZ). (a) The metric of the non-reciprocal Hamiltonian is modified (see text) to absorb the ex- ponential growth of the wavefunction into the coordi- nate, removing the accumulated twisting and restoring a Bloch-wave form via a LST. (b) The Brillouin zone is discretized to incorporate nonreciprocity, defining the ZBZ.

FIG. 2: Schematic of twisting accumulation. The local scaling transformation On (red circle) captures the cu- mulative effect of lattice twisting, which underlies the NHSE.
